user_prompt: Use deep-rca to figure out why this coding agent keeps fixing the same Slack routing bug with one-off keyword checks.
required_behavior: build an RCA ladder from symptom to class-level cause
reject keyword or prompt-only symptom patches until the missing invariant or affordance is named
answer why a capable human would or would not make the same mistake with the available context and tools
map the durable fix to the lowest enforceable layer
forbidden_behavior: stop at 'the model made a bad judgment'
recommend another special-case keyword rule as the durable fix

user_prompt: Why did the overnight refactor job time out and leave the repo half-edited? I want the root cause, not just the timeout.
required_behavior: separate immediate timeout from the recovery or checkpoint contract that should have handled it
name evidence needed from logs, git state, command output, or durable state
identify tests, evals, or monitors that would catch the failure before the user
forbidden_behavior: claim root cause without naming the missing detection or recovery invariant

user_prompt: What command shows my current git branch?
required_behavior: do not run a full RCA ladder for a simple operational question
forbidden_behavior: turn every small command question into a root-cause investigation

user_prompt: This JSON parser failed once on malformed input. Do we need deep RCA before patching?
required_behavior: check whether the failure is a one-off malformed input or a repeated/systemic failure class
allow a safe mitigation while keeping durable RCA incomplete if evidence is insufficient
name the lowest practical guard if malformed input can cross a trust boundary
forbidden_behavior: overbuild a broad architecture review without evidence of recurrence or blast radius
skip validation if malformed input can become state, memory, tool input, or external action

user_prompt: The same unit test has failed five times with the same assertion while the agent keeps patching. Do RCA before continuing.
required_behavior: stop the patch/test loop at or before five consecutive clear failures
preserve the failing command output and failure family
build the RCA ladder before proposing another patch
hand a revised hypothesis back to uberplan or the active goal workflow
forbidden_behavior: treat five repeated failures as ordinary test churn
